ProgramAsWeights (PAW) compiles a short natural-language specification into a tiny neural function that runs locally. Each compiled function is stateless: it takes one text input and returns one text output. It shifts large language models from problem solvers into tool builders, and it is free to use with optional sign-in and rate limits.

NeuralOS is a neural generative model that simulates an entire operating system. Every screen pixel is generated by a neural network, with no real kernel, no installed applications, and no fixed menus or icons. It is a fully generative UI, and the demo shows it can generate frames in response to mouse movement, click, or keypress.

query: give me an overview of neuralos
text: NeuralOS is a neural generative model that simulates an entire operating system. Every screen pixel is generated by a neural network, with no real kernel, no installed applications, and no fixed menus or icons. It is a fully generative UI, and the demo shows it can interact with the screen area and generate the next frame in response to cursor movement.

query: give me an overview of wildchat
text: WildChat is a million-scale dataset of real user-chatbot conversations for studying how people actually use LLMs. The dataset is available on [Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/datasets/yuntian-deng/WildChat-4.8M-Full).

Yuntian won the Laude Institute Slingshots (2026, for NeuralOS), the Argonne National Lab Impact Award (2023), the University of Chicago Rising Stars in Data Science (2022), the ACM Gordon Bell Special Prize (2022), the NVIDIA Fellowship (2021), and the Microsoft Turing Academic Program (2021).

query: how do I interact with it
text: To interact with NeuralOS, move your mouse inside the interaction box and the model generates the next frame in response to where your cursor goes. Click (left-click or right-click) to perform click actions, and use your keyboard to type - just like a real desktop. The page controls allow you to set the number of sampling (diffusion) steps and toggle options such as "Use RNN" and "Auto Input".

query: how does it deal with the mouse cursor
text: NeuralOS encodes the cursor's (x, y) position as a Gaussian spatial map (an extra input channel), which cut cursor error from about 130 pixels to about 1.6 pixels. It uses the mouse movement, click, or keypress to predict the next frame, then feeds that frame back as input.

ProgramAsWeights is a million-scale dataset of real user-chatbot conversations for studying how people actually use LLMs. The dataset is available on [Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/datasets/yuntian-deng/ProgramAsWeights-4.8M-Full).

NeuralOS uses an RNN to track the operating-system state and a diffusion renderer to generate each screen frame. It is autoregressive, so it predicts the next frame from past frames and the user's input, then feeds that frame back as input. The cursor position was a challenge, but encoding it as a Gaussian spatial map cut cursor error from about 130 pixels to about 1.6 pixels.

text: You are looking at NeuralOS, a neural generative model that simulates an entire operating system. Every screen pixel is generated by a neural network, with no real kernel, no installed applications, and no fixed menus or icons.

NeuralOS uses an RNN that tracks the operating-system state (like a kernel) together with a diffusion renderer that generates each screen frame, like a desktop manager. It is autoregressive: given past frames and the user's input, it predicts the next frame, then feeds that frame back as input - responding to every user action, indefinitely, rather than playing a fixed clip.

ProgramAsWeights (PAW) compiles a short natural-language specification into a tiny neural function that runs locally. Each compiled function is stateless: one text input, one text output. It shifts large language models from problem solvers into tool builders for fuzzy text tasks.

WildChat is a million-scale dataset of real user-chatbot conversations for studying how people actually use LLMs. It is available on [Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/datasets/yuntian-deng/WildChat-4.8M-Full) and was featured in the Washington Post.
Yuntian is an Assistant Professor of Computer Science at the University of Waterloo. He is also an Associate at Harvard SEAS and a Faculty Affiliate at the Vector Institute.

query: what kinds of tasks is program as weights good for
